title: Remove secrets from ConfigMap via oslo.config env overrides
description:
# GitHub Issue #224: Remove secrets from ConfigMap by using oslo.config environment variable overrides

**Category**: security | **Scope**: Small

**Description**: `reconcileConfig` (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go:32`) reads the database password from a Kubernetes Secret, constructs the full MySQL connection string (including the password), and embeds it as plaintext in the immutable ConfigMap (`keystone.conf`, `[database] connection`). This means every actor with `get configmap` RBAC can read the database credentials — ConfigMaps have no encryption-at-rest, no separate RBAC boundary, and no audit trail for sensitive data. The admin bootstrap password is already correctly handled via `secretKeyRef` env-var injection (`reconcile_bootstrap.go:124`), and Fernet/credential keys are mounted as Secret volumes — only the database connection string breaks the pattern.

oslo.config has a built-in environment variable override mechanism (merged November 2018, `oslo_config/sources/_environment.py`). It uses the naming convention `OS_<GROUP>__<OPTION>` and overrides config file values with higher priority. This means `OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION` set as an environment variable will automatically take precedence over whatever is in `keystone.conf`.

**Current flow** (problematic):
```
K8s Secret (keystone-db) → Operator reads password → constructs connection string
  → embeds in ConfigMap (keystone.conf [database] connection = mysql+pymysql://user:PASSWORD@host/db)
  → ConfigMap mounted into pod → oslo.config reads from file
```

**Target flow**:
```
K8s Secret (keystone-db) → Operator creates/updates a derived Secret with the full connection string
  → Deployment env: OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef
  → oslo.config picks up env var (higher priority than config file)
  → ConfigMap contains connection string WITHOUT password (or a placeholder)
```

**Motivation**: Kubernetes documentation explicitly states that ConfigMaps are not intended for confidential data. The project already uses External Secrets Operator + OpenBao for secret management and correctly injects the admin password via `secretKeyRef` — the database connection string is the remaining gap. Fixing this aligns all secret handling to a consistent pattern and eliminates plaintext credentials from ConfigMaps.

**Reference**: https://review.opendev.org/c/openstack/oslo.config/+/585850

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go` — stop embedding password in ConfigMap, put placeholder connection string
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go` — add `OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION` env var via `secretKeyRef`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config_test.go` — verify ConfigMap no longer contains password
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go` — verify env var is set
- Optionally: new sub-reconciler or helper to create/maintain the derived connection-string Secret

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] The immutable ConfigMap (`keystone.conf`) no longer contains any password in the `[database] connection` value
- [ ] The Keystone Deployment pod spec includes env var `OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION` sourced from a Kubernetes Secret via `secretKeyRef`
- [ ] oslo.config picks up the env var and overrides the config file value (verified by Keystone successfully connecting to the database)
- [ ] The operator creates/maintains a derived Secret containing the full connection string (username, password, host, port, database) — or reuses the existing DB secret if a single-key layout is feasible
- [ ] Existing `reconcile_config.go` logic for reading the password from the Secret and constructing the URL is moved to wherever the derived Secret is created
- [ ] Unit tests verify: (a) ConfigMap does not contain password, (b) Deployment has correct env var, (c) derived Secret contains valid connection string
- [ ] Admin bootstrap password continues to work unchanged via existing `secretKeyRef` pattern
- [ ] Fernet/credential key handling continues to work unchanged via existing Secret volume mounts

**Labels:** security

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/224

stories.title: Operator removes plaintext database credentials from ConfigMap (CC-0080)
stories.want: the keystone.conf in the operator-managed ConfigMap to contain no database password in the [database] connection value
stories.so_that: actors with only get configmap RBAC cannot read production database credentials
criteria: The rendered keystone.conf in the immutable ConfigMap does not contain the raw password bytes from the keystone-db-credentials Secret
The rendered keystone.conf still contains the [database] section with a connection key, but its value is a non-secret placeholder (no username, no password)
Existing TestReconcileConfig_BrownfieldDatabase and TestReconcileConfig_BasicManagedDatabaseAndCache are updated to assert absence of password and presence of placeholder
No other field under [database] is removed — max_retries and connection_recycle_time remain untouched

stories.title: Operator maintains a derived DB connection Secret (CC-0080)
stories.want: to create and keep up-to-date a Secret containing the full MySQL connection string assembled from spec.database and the ESO-synced DB credentials Secret
stories.so_that: the Deployment and auxiliary pods can consume the connection string via secretKeyRef instead of through the ConfigMap
criteria: A new sub-reconciler (reconcileDBConnectionSecret) creates a Secret named <keystone.Name>-db-connection in the Keystone namespace with owner reference set to the Keystone CR
The Secret contains exactly one data key "connection" whose value is mysql+pymysql://USER:PASSWORD@HOST:PORT/DB?charset=utf8 with proper percent-encoding via url.UserPassword
When the upstream DB credentials Secret changes (username/password), reconcileDBConnectionSecret updates the derived Secret's data key rather than creating a new Secret
When spec.database.host/port/database/clusterRef changes, the derived Secret's connection key is updated on the next reconcile
reconcileDBConnectionSecret runs before reconcileConfig in the main Reconcile loop so that reconcileSecrets and reconcileDeployment can rely on it

stories.title: Keystone API Deployment consumes DB connection string via env var (CC-0080)
stories.want: the Keystone API Deployment pod spec to include env var OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION sourced via secretKeyRef from the derived DB connection Secret
stories.so_that: oslo.config overrides the placeholder in the ConfigMap with the real connection string at runtime
criteria: buildKeystoneDeployment appends an EnvVar entry named OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION with ValueFrom.SecretKeyRef pointing at <keystone.Name>-db-connection, key "connection"
No other env vars are added or removed from the keystone-api container
The existing VolumeMounts for config/fernet-keys/credential-keys remain unchanged
Updated TestBuildKeystoneDeployment asserts presence of the OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION env var with the correct SecretKeyRef

stories.title: Bootstrap, db_sync, and trust-flush jobs consume DB connection via env var (CC-0080)
stories.want: every pod spec that mounts the keystone.conf ConfigMap and requires DB access to include OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef
stories.so_that: all ancillary workloads keep working after the ConfigMap password is replaced with a placeholder
criteria: reconcile_bootstrap.go: Bootstrap Job container env contains OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef to <keystone.Name>-db-connection (key: connection) alongside the existing BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD env
reconcile_database.go buildDBJob: db_sync, expand, migrate, contract, and schema-check Jobs include OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef
reconcile_trustflush.go CronJob container includes OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef
Existing tests for bootstrap/db_sync/trustflush are extended to assert the new env var is present

stories.title: Fernet and credential rotation CronJobs load cleanly without the real DB URL in the config file (CC-0080)
stories.want: the fernet and credential rotation CronJobs to continue loading keystone.conf even though [database] connection is now a placeholder
stories.so_that: rotation jobs do not fail because oslo.config sees an unresolved placeholder
criteria: The fernet rotation CronJob container gets OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef (same secret, key: connection) so oslo.config parses cleanly
The credential rotation CronJob container gets OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef
Existing fernet/credential rotation tests are extended to assert the new env var
Manual verification: fernet/credential e2e tests continue to pass against an upgraded operator

stories.title: reconcileSecrets gates on the derived DB connection Secret (CC-0080)
stories.want: the SecretsReady condition to flip False with reason WaitingForDBConnection when the derived <keystone.Name>-db-connection Secret is missing or lacks the connection key
stories.so_that: status clearly signals which secret is not yet available
criteria: reconcileSecrets (or reconcileDBConnectionSecret itself) verifies the derived Secret is present with key "connection"
When the upstream DB credentials Secret is missing, DBConnectionSecret creation short-circuits with a descriptive condition message rather than creating an invalid Secret
No other existing SecretsReady transitions (WaitingForDBCredentials, WaitingForAdminCredentials, SecretStoreNotReady) are altered

stories.title: E2E test confirms ConfigMap is free of secrets and Keystone still reaches the DB (CC-0080)
stories.want: an end-to-end chainsaw test that deploys Keystone, reads the generated ConfigMap, and asserts the password is absent while the pod is Ready
stories.so_that: the security invariant is automatically enforced in CI and cannot regress silently
criteria: tests/e2e/keystone/configmap-no-secrets/chainsaw-test.yaml exists and is referenced from tests/e2e/keystone/chainsaw-test-list (or equivalent Make target)
The test deploys a minimal Keystone CR (reuse basic-deployment fixtures), then asserts the keystone.conf key of the generated ConfigMap matches a regex that forbids the DB password value
The test asserts the Keystone Deployment has env var OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION with ValueFrom.SecretKeyRef
The test asserts the Keystone CR's Ready condition becomes True (proves oslo.config picked up the env var and the pod connected)

stories.title: Reference documentation captures the new secret-handling pattern (CC-0080)
stories.want: the reference docs to explain how the operator creates the derived connection Secret and how oslo.config env-var overrides are used
stories.so_that: future contributors understand why [database] connection is a placeholder and do not revert the change
criteria: docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md gains a subsection describing the derived <name>-db-connection Secret and the OS_<GROUP>__<OPTION> env var override pattern (CC-0080)
The subsection references the upstream oslo.config change (https://review.opendev.org/c/openstack/oslo.config/+/585850)
Existing schema-drift/e2e reference docs remain unchanged

requirements.description: The operator SHALL render the keystone.conf ConfigMap such that the [database] connection value contains no plaintext password, user, or host from the DB credentials Secret
requirements.rationale: ConfigMaps have no encryption-at-rest and a separate RBAC boundary than Secrets; embedding credentials violates the project's secret-handling policy (CC-0080).

scenarios.name: Brownfield mode produces placeholder connection
scenarios.when: reconcileConfig runs with spec.database.host set and a DB credentials Secret containing password="ks_pass"
scenarios.then: the rendered keystone.conf's [database] connection value does not contain the substring "ks_pass"
and_then: the connection value is exactly the placeholder (e.g. mysql+pymysql://placeholder) defined by a package-level const
[database] max_retries and connection_recycle_time keys remain present and unchanged

scenarios.name: Managed mode produces placeholder connection
scenarios.when: reconcileConfig runs with spec.database.clusterRef set and a DB credentials Secret containing password="secret123"
scenarios.then: the rendered keystone.conf does not contain "secret123" anywhere
and_then: the cluster hostname "mariadb-cluster.default.svc:3306/keystone" is NOT present in keystone.conf (moved to the derived Secret)
the placeholder is the exact same value as in brownfield mode

scenarios.name: Special characters in credentials do not leak
scenarios.when: reconcileConfig runs with a password containing ":", "@", or "/"
scenarios.then: no percent-encoded or raw form of those credentials appears in keystone.conf
and_then: the special-character encoding logic moves unchanged to the derived-Secret reconciler

requirements.description: The operator SHALL create and maintain a derived Secret named <keystone.Name>-db-connection containing a single key "connection" whose value is the fully assembled mysql+pymysql URL
requirements.rationale: Secrets provide the correct RBAC boundary and encryption envelope for credential material; a single canonical Secret simplifies Deployment/Job wiring.

scenarios.name: First reconcile creates the Secret
scenarios.when: reconcileDBConnectionSecret runs and no prior <keystone.Name>-db-connection Secret exists
scenarios.then: a Secret is created in keystone.Namespace with owner reference to the Keystone CR and data["connection"] equal to the assembled URL
and_then: controllerutil.SetControllerReference marks the Keystone CR as controller

scenarios.name: Password rotation updates the derived Secret
scenarios.when: the upstream DB credentials Secret's password value changes
scenarios.then: the next reconcile updates data["connection"] to reflect the new password without changing the Secret's name or owner reference
and_then: the Secret's Data map has exactly one key "connection" (no orphan keys from previous reconciles)

scenarios.name: Upstream DB credentials Secret missing
scenarios.when: reconcileDBConnectionSecret runs while spec.database.secretRef.Name does not exist
scenarios.then: the function returns a ctrl.Result with RequeueAfter=RequeueSecretPolling and sets SecretsReady=False with a descriptive reason
and_then: no <keystone.Name>-db-connection Secret is created with stale or empty values

requirements.description: The operator SHALL inject env var OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via SecretKeyRef pointing at <keystone.Name>-db-connection key "connection" into the keystone-api Deployment container
requirements.rationale: oslo.config's OS_<GROUP>__<OPTION> mechanism overrides file-based config at runtime, so the placeholder in the ConfigMap is replaced with the real URL only in-process.

scenarios.name: Env var present on Deployment container
scenarios.then: the returned Deployment's spec.template.spec.containers[keystone-api].env contains an EnvVar with Name=OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION and ValueFrom.SecretKeyRef.Name=<keystone.Name>-db-connection, Key=connection
and_then: no plain Value is set on that EnvVar (ValueFrom only)

scenarios.name: No other env vars are added or removed
scenarios.then: env contains exactly the new OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION entry (the keystone-api container previously had no env)

scenarios.name: Optional field key presence
scenarios.then: Optional field is nil (the Secret is required; missing Secret must fail the pod)

requirements.description: The operator SHALL inject OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION into every Job/CronJob pod spec that mounts the keystone.conf ConfigMap: bootstrap, db_sync, expand, migrate, contract, schema-check, trust-flush, fernet-rotate, credential-rotate
requirements.rationale: Any pod that loads keystone.conf via oslo.config must see a valid connection URL; leaving the placeholder in place would break DB-dependent commands and may break config parsing for DB-independent commands.

scenarios.name: Bootstrap Job has env var
scenarios.then: the returned Job's container.Env includes OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef to <keystone.Name>-db-connection alongside the existing BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD env

scenarios.name: DB sync family of Jobs has env var
scenarios.when: buildDBJob is invoked for db-sync, db-expand, db-migrate, db-contract, or db-schema-check
scenarios.then: the returned Job's container.Env includes OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef

scenarios.name: Trust-flush CronJob has env var
scenarios.then: the returned CronJob's container.Env includes OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef

scenarios.name: Fernet and credential rotation CronJobs have env var
scenarios.when: the rotation CronJob pod spec is built
scenarios.then: env includes OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef so oslo.config loads cleanly even if the job itself does not use the DB

requirements.description: reconcileDBConnectionSecret SHALL run before reconcileConfig in the main Reconcile loop and its failures SHALL be surfaced via the SecretsReady condition
requirements.rationale: The Deployment references the derived Secret by name; the Secret must exist before the Deployment is created so that pod creation does not fail with MissingSecret.

scenarios.name: Ordering in Reconcile
scenarios.then: reconcileDBConnectionSecret is invoked after reconcileSecrets (which verifies upstream ESO Secrets) and before reconcileConfig
and_then: no existing sub-reconciler call order is changed beyond the insertion point

scenarios.name: Failure propagates to condition
scenarios.when: reconcileDBConnectionSecret returns an error or non-zero Result
scenarios.then: updateStatus is called with the SecretsReady condition updated and the error propagated
and_then: subsequent sub-reconcilers (reconcileConfig, reconcileDatabase, reconcileDeployment) do not run

requirements.description: The operator SHALL watch the derived <keystone.Name>-db-connection Secret for changes and requeue the owning Keystone CR when it is modified or deleted
requirements.rationale: If the Secret is deleted out-of-band (e.g. by an admin), the operator must recreate it without waiting for the periodic requeue.

scenarios.name: Owned Secret watch already covers it
scenarios.then: the existing secretToKeystoneMapper matches <keystone.Name>-db-connection because it is owned by the Keystone CR (ownerReference UID match via isOwnedBy)
and_then: no new Watches call is required; the existing owner-reference based mapping is sufficient

scenarios.name: Secret deletion triggers recreate
scenarios.when: the derived Secret is deleted while the Keystone CR is Ready
scenarios.then: the next reconcile recreates the Secret with identical data
and_then: Deployment pods that were evicted during that window restart cleanly once the Secret is recreated

requirements.description: The operator SHALL preserve all existing secret-handling paths (admin bootstrap password, fernet keys, credential keys) without modification
requirements.rationale: The feature scope is narrowly the DB connection string; unrelated secret flows must not be touched to minimize blast radius.

scenarios.name: BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD env unchanged
scenarios.then: BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD env var continues to come from secretKeyRef on keystone.Spec.Bootstrap.AdminPasswordSecretRef with key=password

scenarios.name: Fernet/credential volume mounts unchanged
scenarios.then: fernet-keys and credential-keys VolumeMounts and Volumes remain exactly as before

requirements.description: The placeholder connection string in the ConfigMap SHOULD be a syntactically valid PyMySQL URL that clearly signals "placeholder" to a human reader
requirements.rationale: A valid URL lets oslo.config parse the file cleanly even before the env-var override is applied; a recognizable placeholder aids debugging if the env var is ever missing.

scenarios.name: Placeholder is valid URL
scenarios.when: config.RenderINI is invoked on the merged map
scenarios.then: the [database] connection value parses as a URL via url.Parse without error
and_then: the host portion is a literal like "placeholder" or "overridden-by-env"

scenarios.name: Placeholder exported as constant
scenarios.when: the placeholder string is referenced from tests
scenarios.then: a single exported/unexported package-level const dbConnectionPlaceholder is used (no string duplication)

requirements.description: Unit and integration tests SHALL assert (a) the ConfigMap contains no password, (b) the derived Secret contains the correct URL, and (c) every affected pod spec has the OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION env var
requirements.rationale: Regression coverage is required because the security invariant cannot be restored by reading logs after it is broken.

scenarios.name: ConfigMap absence test
scenarios.then: it asserts keystone.conf does NOT contain "ks_pass" and DOES contain the placeholder

scenarios.name: Derived Secret content test
scenarios.then: it asserts the Secret's data["connection"] equals the expected URL produced by url.UserPassword with proper encoding
and_then: it tests both brownfield and managed modes
it tests that a changed password updates the Secret in place

scenarios.name: Deployment env var test
scenarios.then: it asserts env contains OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via SecretKeyRef to <name>-db-connection

scenarios.name: Job env var tests
scenarios.then: each asserts OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION is present via secretKeyRef

requirements.description: The operator MUST NOT introduce a new ESO ExternalSecret or PushSecret for the derived connection Secret — it is materialized directly by the operator from the existing upstream Secret
requirements.rationale: The upstream credential is already an ExternalSecret from OpenBao; adding a second ESO hop would duplicate secret management and add a failure mode. The derived Secret is a derived materialization, not a separate source of truth.

scenarios.name: No ESO artefacts created
scenarios.then: it creates only a corev1.Secret (no esov1.ExternalSecret or esov1alpha1.PushSecret)
and_then: IsClusterSecretStoreReady is not consulted for the derived Secret

scenarios.name: Garbage collection on CR deletion
scenarios.then: the derived Secret is garbage-collected by Kubernetes via ownerReference (no explicit finalizer needed)

tasks.title: Add package-level const dbConnectionPlaceholder (e.g. "mysql+pymysql://placeholder") to operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_config.go and reference it in the [database] connection default (CC-0080, REQ-001, REQ-008). Keep the existing {{DB_CONNECTION}} placeholder mechanism intact for other use cases.

Create operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_dbconnection_secret.go with reconcileDBConnectionSecret(ctx, keystone) that reads the upstream DB credentials Secret, builds the mysql+pymysql URL using the existing url.UserPassword/resolveDatabaseHost/dbPort helpers (moved verbatim from reconcile_config.go), and creates or updates a corev1.Secret named <keystone.Name>-db-connection with data["connection"] (CC-0080, REQ-002, REQ-010). Use controllerutil.SetControllerReference and a Get-then-Create-or-Update pattern mirroring secrets.EnsurePushSecret.

Write unit tests in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_dbconnection_secret_test.go for: creation in brownfield mode, creation in managed mode, password-rotation update-in-place, upstream-missing requeue, and absence of ESO artefacts (CC-0080, REQ-002, REQ-009, REQ-010).

Modify reconcileConfig in reconcile_config.go to drop password/username reads and connection-URL construction; set [database] connection to dbConnectionPlaceholder via the config.InjectSecrets pipeline with DB_CONNECTION mapping to the placeholder (CC-0080, REQ-001). Keep all other sections byte-identical.

Update existing reconcile_config_test.go expectations: replace assertions that look for mysql+pymysql://...@... with assertions that the placeholder is present and the password substring is absent; add a regression test TestReconcileConfig_SpecialCharactersInCredentials_DoNotLeakToConfigMap (CC-0080, REQ-001, REQ-009).

Wire reconcileDBConnectionSecret into keystone_controller.go Reconcile between reconcileSecrets and reconcileConfig, propagating result/error via updateStatus; update subConditionTypes only if a new condition type is introduced (prefer reusing SecretsReady) (CC-0080, REQ-005).

Extend keystone_controller_test.go to cover: ordering of derived Secret creation before ConfigMap creation, SecretsReady=False propagation when upstream Secret is missing, and secretToKeystoneMapper enqueueing on derived-Secret changes via ownerReference (CC-0080, REQ-005, REQ-006).

Add a small helper buildDBConnectionEnvVar(keystone) *corev1.EnvVar in reconcile_deployment.go returning the OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION EnvVar with ValueFrom.SecretKeyRef=<name>-db-connection, Key=connection (CC-0080, REQ-003). Use it from every pod-spec builder to avoid string duplication.

Modify buildKeystoneDeployment in reconcile_deployment.go to append the OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION env via the new helper; keep VolumeMounts/Volumes untouched (CC-0080, REQ-003, REQ-007). Update TestBuildKeystoneDeployment with the env-var assertion.

Modify reconcile_bootstrap.go buildBootstrapJob to append the OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION env var alongside BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD; update reconcile_bootstrap_test.go to assert both envs present and unchanged ordering (CC-0080, REQ-004, REQ-007, REQ-009).

Modify reconcile_database.go buildDBJob to append OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION on the container; verify db_sync, expand, migrate, contract, and schema-check all inherit it. Extend reconcile_database_test.go accordingly (CC-0080, REQ-004, REQ-009).
Modify reconcile_trustflush.go CronJob container spec to append OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION; update reconcile_trustflush_test.go (CC-0080, REQ-004, REQ-009).

Modify reconcile_fernet.go fernet-rotate CronJob container Env to append OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION alongside the existing OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys; update reconcile_fernet_test.go to assert both envs (CC-0080, REQ-004, REQ-009).
Modify reconcile_credential.go credential-rotate CronJob container Env similarly; update reconcile_credential_test.go (CC-0080, REQ-004, REQ-009).

Add envtest integration test TestIntegration_KeystonePodReachesDatabaseViaEnvOverride in operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go that reconciles a Keystone CR and asserts: placeholder in ConfigMap, env var on Deployment, and valid URL in the derived Secret (CC-0080, REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003).
Add integration test TestIntegration_RecreateDerivedSecretWhenDeleted that deletes the derived Secret and expects re-creation on the next reconcile (CC-0080, REQ-006).

Create tests/e2e/keystone/configmap-no-secrets/chainsaw-test.yaml (+ 00-keystone-cr.yaml fixture) that applies a minimal Keystone CR, waits for Ready=True, asserts via kubectl+jq that keystone.conf contains the placeholder and not the DB password, and that the Deployment env contains OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION (CC-0080, REQ-001, REQ-003).
Register tests/e2e/keystone/configmap-no-secrets in the existing chainsaw test list / Make target so CI picks it up (CC-0080).

Extend docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md with a new section "Database credentials and oslo.config env-var overrides (CC-0080)" that documents the <name>-db-connection Secret, the OS_<GROUP>__<OPTION> pattern, and cites https://review.opendev.org/c/openstack/oslo.config/+/585850. Keep the existing TOC and other sections untouched.

test_specifications.story: Operator removes plaintext database credentials from ConfigMap (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: keystone.conf does NOT contain "ks_pass" and DOES contain the placeholder connection string exactly once

test_specifications.story: Operator removes plaintext database credentials from ConfigMap (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: keystone.conf does NOT contain "secret123" or the managed cluster DSN host path

test_specifications.story: Operator removes plaintext database credentials from ConfigMap (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: percent-encoded and raw forms of user@domain:p@ss:w/rd are both absent from keystone.conf; they appear only in the derived Secret

test_specifications.story: Operator maintains a derived DB connection Secret (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: Secret <name>-db-connection created with owner ref and data[connection] == mysql+pymysql://ks_user:ks_pass@db.example.com:3306/keystone?charset=utf8

test_specifications.story: Operator maintains a derived DB connection Secret (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: Secret data[connection] uses <crname>@<clusterref>.<namespace>.svc:3306/<db>?charset=utf8

test_specifications.story: Operator maintains a derived DB connection Secret (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: changing the upstream password from "old" to "new" causes data[connection] to be updated in place; Secret Name and UID remain stable

test_specifications.story: Operator maintains a derived DB connection Secret (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: returns ctrl.Result{RequeueAfter: RequeueSecretPolling} and sets SecretsReady=False with reason WaitingForDBCredentials; no derived Secret is created

test_specifications.story: Keystone API Deployment consumes DB connection string via env var (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: deploy.Spec.Template.Spec.Containers[0].Env contains EnvVar Name=OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION with ValueFrom.SecretKeyRef.Name=<name>-db-connection, Key=connection; no plain Value set

test_specifications.story: Fernet and credential rotation CronJobs load cleanly without the real DB URL in the config file (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: fernet-keys and credential-keys VolumeMounts and Volumes are present with identical parameters as before (regression guard)

test_specifications.story: Bootstrap, db_sync, and trust-flush jobs consume DB connection via env var (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: bootstrap container.Env contains BOTH BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD (unchanged) and OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef

test_specifications.story: Bootstrap, db_sync, and trust-flush jobs consume DB connection via env var (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: db_sync/expand/migrate/contract/schema-check container.Env contains OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef

test_specifications.story: Bootstrap, db_sync, and trust-flush jobs consume DB connection via env var (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: trust-flush CronJob container.Env contains OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef

test_specifications.story: Fernet and credential rotation CronJobs load cleanly without the real DB URL in the config file (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: fernet-rotate CronJob container.Env contains OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef; pre-existing OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys env remains

test_specifications.story: Fernet and credential rotation CronJobs load cleanly without the real DB URL in the config file (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: credential-rotate CronJob container.Env contains OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef

test_specifications.story: reconcileSecrets gates on the derived DB connection Secret (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: after a single Reconcile invocation on a healthy CR, the <name>-db-connection Secret exists BEFORE the keystone.conf ConfigMap is created (verified by inspecting fake client object ordering via UIDs or by asserting both exist after reconcile)

test_specifications.story: reconcileSecrets gates on the derived DB connection Secret (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: when the upstream DB credentials Secret is deleted, the next Reconcile sets SecretsReady=False with an informative reason and does not create or update Deployment/ConfigMap

test_specifications.story: Operator maintains a derived DB connection Secret (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: the existing secretToKeystoneMapper enqueues the owning Keystone CR when the derived <name>-db-connection Secret (carrying an ownerReference) is modified

test_specifications.story: Operator maintains a derived DB connection Secret (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: after reconcile, no ExternalSecret or PushSecret objects exist for the derived connection Secret name

test_specifications.story: Keystone API Deployment consumes DB connection string via env var (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: envtest-based integration test: reconcile a Keystone CR, confirm the ConfigMap has the placeholder, confirm the Deployment has the env var sourced from <name>-db-connection, confirm the derived Secret's data[connection] is a valid URL

test_specifications.story: Operator maintains a derived DB connection Secret (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: delete the derived Secret and trigger a reconcile; assert the Secret is recreated with the same owner reference and data

test_specifications.story: E2E test confirms ConfigMap is free of secrets and Keystone still reaches the DB (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: chainsaw test deploys minimal Keystone CR, asserts keystone.conf in the generated ConfigMap does not contain the DB password (script check), asserts Deployment has OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION env var, asserts Keystone Ready=True

test_specifications.story: E2E test confirms ConfigMap is free of secrets and Keystone still reaches the DB (CC-0080)
test_specifications.expected: chainsaw assert step verifies the Keystone API Deployment has env var OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION sourced from secretKeyRef and the pod reaches Ready state

review_criteria: [Blocker] No rendered keystone.conf in any reconciler output contains a password byte (grep-based test passes)
[Blocker] Deployment, bootstrap Job, every db_sync flavour, trust-flush CronJob, fernet-rotate CronJob, and credential-rotate CronJob all have OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION env var via secretKeyRef
[Blocker] reconcileDBConnectionSecret runs before reconcileConfig and its failures surface via SecretsReady condition
[Blocker] Derived <name>-db-connection Secret has owner reference to the Keystone CR (garbage-collected on deletion; no finalizer required)
[Critical] All existing reconcile_config_test.go cases pass after adjustments; no assertions are silently weakened
[Critical] Special-character encoding logic (url.UserPassword) is preserved exactly once, now in the derived-Secret reconciler
[Critical] Chainsaw e2e test tests/e2e/keystone/configmap-no-secrets passes in CI and is wired into the test list
[Minor] docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md documents the new pattern and cites the upstream oslo.config change
[Minor] No new ESO ExternalSecret or PushSecret resources are introduced for the derived Secret
[Minor] No unused code or dead constants remain after removing password reads from reconcile_config.go
implementation_notes:
Architecture: the operator gains one new sub-reconciler (reconcileDBConnectionSecret) placed between reconcileSecrets and reconcileConfig. It reads keystone.Spec.Database.SecretRef (materialized by ESO), builds the mysql+pymysql URL using the existing resolveDatabaseHost/dbPort helpers and url.UserPassword for percent-encoding, and writes/updates a corev1.Secret named <keystone.Name>-db-connection with owner reference to the Keystone CR. The existing reconcile_config.go loses the password read and URL construction, and [database] connection becomes a placeholder constant (dbConnectionPlaceholder). Every pod spec that mounts the keystone.conf ConfigMap gains an env var OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION via secretKeyRef to <name>-db-connection key=connection — oslo.config's environment-variable override picks it up at runtime and supersedes the placeholder. No ESO ExternalSecret is created for the derived Secret; it is a pure materialization from existing inputs. Garbage collection is handled by Kubernetes via ownerReference.

Potential pitfalls: (1) the Keystone Deployment currently has no Env entries on the keystone-api container, so the new helper buildDBConnectionEnvVar returns a value (not a pointer) appended into a fresh []corev1.EnvVar — be careful to initialize, not to dereference nil; (2) the fernet and credential rotation CronJobs already set oslo env overrides (OS_fernet_tokens__max_active_keys) — append, do not replace; (3) do not remove the {{DB_CONNECTION}} placeholder-injection mechanism in config.InjectSecrets — it is still useful for clarity and may be reused; (4) the e2e assertion must grep the rendered keystone.conf inside the Pod (via kubectl exec or kubectl get cm -o jsonpath) rather than trusting the operator's in-memory view.

ASCII Architecture Diagram (components affected):

    +------------------+          +------------------+         +----------------------+
    | ESO ExternalSecret|--(sync)->| Secret           |--(read)>| reconcileDBConnection|
    | keystone-db      |          | keystone-db-     |         |   Secret             |
    +------------------+          | credentials      |         +----------+-----------+
                                  +------------------+                    |
                                                                           v
    +-----------------------------------------------+        +-------------------------+
    | Deployment / Job / CronJob pod spec           |<-------| Secret                  |
    | env: OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION (secretKeyRef)   |        | <name>-db-connection    |
    +-----------------------+-----------------------+        | data: connection=...    |
                            |                                +-------------------------+
                            v
                 +---------------------+
                 | oslo.config         |
                 | env override > file |
                 +---------------------+

Data Flow Diagram:

    CRD.spec.database          ESO-synced Secret            derived Secret           env var
  (host/port/db/clusterRef)  (username/password)  -->  (mysql+pymysql://...)  -->  OS_DATABASE__CONNECTION
             \____________________________/                    |                            |
                           |                                   v                            v
                 reconcileDBConnectionSecret            [referenced by]        [read by oslo.config]
                           |                            Deployment,Jobs,        inside Keystone process
                           v                            CronJobs
                (derived Secret written)

    ConfigMap (keystone.conf) [database] connection = mysql+pymysql://placeholder
       └── oslo.config file layer, overridden by env var at runtime.

Existing patterns to follow: secrets.GetSecretValue (Secret reads), controllerutil.SetControllerReference (ownership), secrets.EnsurePushSecret (Create/Update-in-place idiom — adapt for corev1.Secret since we do not need a PushSecret here), conditions.SetCondition for SecretsReady, and secretToKeystoneMapper already re-enqueues on owned-Secret changes via isOwnedBy.
